Court Reporter: Diane L. Freeman, CSR #5884
AFTER HEARING, TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff City and County of San Francisco's motion to compel further responses to request for production of documents is
Granted in part and denied in part. The motion is moot as to requests #: 19-21, 24, 26-29 as defendant has agreed to produce responsive documents. The motion is moot as to requests #: 15, 18, and 30 as defendant has stated that there are no documents. Defendant shall prepare a separate declaration under penalty of perjury verifying said representation.

The motion is granted as to requests: 1 - 5, 7-12, 14, 16, 17, 22, 23, 25 provided that
Counsel for either party may not disclose, or permit to be disclosed, any documents produced in response to the requests for purposes unrelated to the instant litigation or any administrative proceedings unless specifically permitted to do so by the court after a hearing and a showing of good cause.

Notwithstanding the above, counsel for either party may disclose or permit to be disclosed the records produced in discovery to persons employed by counsel to assist in the preparation of the case, if that disclosure is required for that preparation. Persons provided this information by counsel shall be informed that further dissemination of the information constitutes a violation of a court order.
As to requests #6 and 13, the motion is granted in part at this time subject to the terms of the protective order set forth above. Defendant is ordered to produce responsive documents that are specific to the project. Defendant may redact information unrelated to the project at issue. Parties to submit Protective Order. Compliance by 2/25/08. Commissioner Bruce Chan.
